published_prior_art.cite: WangLiYu2026, DobribanYu2025, and HemerikGoeman2018 give adjacent causal-prediction or symmetry machinery, not this universal two-arm selector-mass iff.
RamdasBarberCandesTibshirani2023 supports arbitrary permutation reference distributions; the displayed LP feasibility equivalence is elementary but its common-target application is distinct.
TibshiraniBarberCandesRamdas2019 supplies the finite-sample weighted-conformal likelihood-ratio construction; the proposed orbit quotient is a specialized causal instantiation.
BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023 already develops coverage-robustness bounds under nonexchangeability; the orbit-mass specialization is narrower.
Standard split-conformal rank inversion already makes the k=M+1 infinite-threshold boundary immediate.
Weighted-conformal support mismatch is a known failure mode; the bounded two-arm orbit witness is a useful specialization, not an independently broad new principle.
BaiLiuShaikhTabordMeehan2024 supplies matched-pair randomization; this is an algebraic reduction of the proposed selector construction.
No checked paper states the exact common-target two-arm finite orbit frontier.

structure_flags.label: Proposal kernel
structure_flags.one_line: The tldr and strongest computable claim are the weighted-coverage and orbit-balance theorems, while the only OEQ is a secondary sharpness refinement; relabel the central unproved theorem as a conjecture or promote the frontier OEQ to the headline.

novelty_flags.label: Theorems 1 and 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits LeiCandès2021, the direct finite-sample causal/conformal ITE comparator for randomized experiments, and does not explain precisely why its individual-effect coverage construction fails to subsume the proposed matched-block selector regime.

soundness_flags.label: Target-selection and predictor definitions
soundness_flags.one_line: The target draw is stated independent only of replicated randomization draws, not of the latent arrays, so q_tar alone does not determine the target residual law; additionally mhat_a is defined as a function trained only on T but its equivariance condition evaluates it as a function of O_i. State T independent of all scored latent arrays (or give its conditional kernel) and make the predictor's data inputs consistent.

soundness_flags.label: ass:known-matched-randomization and ass:randomization-unconfoundedness
soundness_flags.one_line: The fixed conditional law L(Z_i|L_i)=nu already implies independence of Z_i from the complete latent array, hence implies the separately listed conditional randomization-unconfoundedness condition.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: All finite-coverage statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Coverage is conditional on an iid superpopulation sequence of replicated matched-block arrays, independent training, and prespecified selector weights; it is not a finite-population guarantee for the single Shamba Maisha trial.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Exact finite-sample marginal coverage holds only under the replicated-array conditional regime after the target-selection independence is explicitly stated.

source_verification_receipts.version: arXiv preprint 2401.01977; journal version not inspected
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper presents finite-sample conformal causal prediction intervals for cluster and individual treatment effects with arbitrary working outcome models.
The article concerns exactness of random-permutation tests under group-based permutation methodology.
It gives weighted conformal prediction under known test-to-training likelihood ratios and uses an infinity-augmented quantile construction.
It validates randomized permutation p-values with arbitrary permutation distributions and distinguishes them from randomization tests.
It studies conformal coverage robustness when exchangeability fails, including weighted-quantile methods.
The paper studies cluster randomized matched pairs with one cluster randomly selected for treatment within each pair, including randomization-test validity for specified nulls.

source_verification_receipts.version: JAMA Network Open 5, e2246158; SSRN record
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Shamba Maisha randomized eight matched pairs of health facilities to intervention or control.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Make the central weighted-orbit finite-coverage result a conjecture and add a one-paragraph route identifying the weighted-exchangeability lemma to be adapted; retain the LP and pair calculations as supporting propositions.

Repair the target sampling law by declaring T independent of all L_i,Z_i, or replace q_tar with an explicit observed-covariate-dependent target kernel and derive the corresponding target-to-calibration weights.

Add Lei and Candès (JRSSB 2021) to related work and state a strict comparison: its randomized-experiment ITE construction versus the new matched-block orbit-selector regime, including the condition under which the two agree or differ.

Advance the OEQ into a concrete conjecture for a nontrivial two-orbit configuration, with an explicit finite-LP candidate frontier and a least-favourable orbit-score family; this would turn the currently incremental robustness bound into a sharper field-level focal result.

recommended_next_step: Revise the target/predictor probability model, remove the redundant ignorability assumption, and recast the central open weighted-orbit claim as a conjecture after adding the omitted causal-conformal comparator.
